name: Sierras de Burete, Lavia y Cambrón - North Sierra Espuña — return from Bullas
name_original: Sierras de Burete, Lavia y Cambrón - Norte Sierra Espuña — vuelta desde Bullas
region_intro: Road cycling around Bullas, located in the Region of Murcia, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The terrain features mountains reaching around 1,000 meters, cultivated plains with vineyards and almond trees, and river ecosystems. Cyclists can find routes that traverse well-paved surfaces, quiet country roads, and sections of the Vía Verde del Noroeste, a scenic rails-to-trails path. This varied topography provides options for different fitness levels, from moderate to very good, with routes featuring significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Bullas The most popular road cycling route is Sierras de Burete, Lavia y Cambrón - North Sierra Espuña — return from Bullas, a 48.7 miles (78.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging climbs through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Usero Waterfall – Salto del Usero loop from Bullas, a moderate 43.6 miles (70.2 km) path. This route passes by the Salto del Usero, a notable geological feature on the River Mula. Local road cyclists also love the Alfonso XIII Dam – Alfonso XIII Reservoir loop from Bullas, a 47.4 miles (76.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Bullas is defined by mountainous areas, extensive vineyards, and river valleys like the River Mula. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Bullas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bullas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Bullas?
A: Bullas offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll encounter mountainous areas reaching around 1,000 meters, cultivated plains with extensive vineyards and almond trees, and river valleys like the River Mula. Routes often feature a mix of well-paved surfaces and quiet country roads, providing varied cycling experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bullas?
A: Yes, Bullas offers routes for various fitness levels. While many routes involve significant elevation gains, you can find easier options. For a gentler experience, consider sections of the Vía Verde del Noroeste, a scenic rails-to-trails path that provides a relatively flat and picturesque ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge will find several demanding routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009738587/sierras-de-burete-lavia-y-cambron-north-sierra-espuna-return-from-bullas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Burete, Lavia y Cambrón - North Sierra Espuña — return from Bullas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 78.4 km route with nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14625716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argos Reservoir – Castillo de Don Fabio loop from Bullas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 95.7 km with over 1,000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Bullas?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Bullas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14625261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall – Salto del Usero loop from Bullas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Dam – Alfonso XIII Reservoir loop from Bullas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Bullas is rich in natural beauty. Many routes pass by or near significant features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a stunning geological formation on the River Mula. You can also cycle through areas with lush riverbank vegetation, extensive vineyards, and dramatic mountainous landscapes like Garci-Sánchez and El Castellar.

Q: Is there a dedicated bike path or greenway for road cycling in Bullas?
A: Yes, a significant draw for cyclists is the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Noroeste&lt;/strong&gt;, a 'rails-to-trails' bike path that follows an old railway line. This greenway offers a gentle and scenic cycling experience, passing through charming villages and diverse landscapes. A notable section is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4137013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Noroeste: Bullas to Cehegín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bullas?
A: The region of Murcia generally enjoys a mild climate, making spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer, which can be quite warm, and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months. However, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is popular for swimming in summer, indicating that some areas are enjoyable even then.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: Yes, especially along popular routes and greenways. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3226400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cehegín Station – Restaurant and Gelateria on the Northwest Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offers a convenient stop for food and drinks if you're cycling that section of the Vía Verde del Noroeste.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take around Bullas?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. You can find shorter routes under 2 hours, while more challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40346009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Alfonso XIII Reservoir loop from Bullas&lt;/a&gt; (112.7 km with over 1,300m elevation), can take over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Bullas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic greenways, and the beautiful natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Usero&lt;/a&gt;. The mix of well-paved roads and quiet country lanes also contributes to a highly enjoyable experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bullas?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Bullas documented on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.
tips: Historic milestone dating from the 18th century that delimits the point where the municipalities of Totana, Mula and Lorca meet.

The C5 highway runs along the entire north face of Sierra Espuña, from Pliego passing through Casas Nuevas and connecting with the MU-503. It is an up and down road with little traffic surrounded, in spring, by green fields.

Located in the Northwest region, the municipality of Bullas offers various points of interest, such as the Parish Church of Nuestra Señora del Rosario (declared an Asset of Cultural Interest) and the Salto del Usero, ideal for bathing in summer, located on the outskirts of the location.

waypoints.name: Historic Boundary Marker of Puerto de Lorca
Parish Church of Our Lady of the Rosary, Bullas
